items.title: 'It's quite barren': SA fishers demand action to protect stocks

children.content: Easter is a popular time for consumers to purchase fish, but commercial fishers in South Australia say stocks have been decimated by the algal bloom. They fear it will get worse without further action.

gallery.altText: A South Australian commercial fisher in fishing clothes.

thumbnail.altText: A South Australian commercial fisher in fishing clothes.

items.title: How an Australian Sex Party candidate became a One Nation MP

children.content: Sixteen years before Jason Virgo won the SA seat of MacKillop for One Nation in the state's south-east, he twice ran for parliament as an Australian Sex Party candidate.

image.altText: Jason Virgo in a business shirt smiles and stands in front of other One Nation MPs

items.title: Motorcyclist killed in traffic light crash in Adelaide, another rushed to hospital

children.content: A motorcyclist has died at Ovingham in Adelaide's inner north, hours after another rider was flown to hospital with life-threatening injuries sustained during a separate crash.

image.altText: An SA Police vehicle with flashing lights.

items.title: SA Electoral Commission apologises to staff for post-election pay delay

children.content: The Electoral Commission of South Australia has apologised to staff who have not been fully paid almost a fortnight after the state election, saying a payroll processing delay had caused the problem.

gallery.altText: An electoral officer at a polling station.

thumbnail.altText: An electoral officer at a polling station.

items.title: Pedestrian fatally hit by bus could have been retrieving item, SA Police say

children.content: Witnesses are being encouraged to seek counselling after a 39-year-old died when she was hit by an Adelaide Metro bus on King William Road in the Adelaide CBD. It comes after two other pedestrians were hit and injured in similar incidents earlier in the week.

image.altText: A red Adelaide Metro bus on a road in Adelaide's CBD.

items.title: Walker blasts teammates as Dockers dominates Crows after Blues meltdown

children.content: Fremantle flexes its muscles in the first half against an undermanned Adelaide side after North Melbourne overturns a 22-point deficit to beat Carlton. Follow live.

image.altText: An AFL player in white holds a yellow ball while a player in blue tackles from behind

items.title: Adelaide marks one year of no cars crashing on O-Bahn

children.content: The Adelaide busway has remained clear of crashed cars for a whole year.

image.altText: A hatchback crashed on a concrete bus while a bus goes in the other direction

items.title: One Nation wins last SA lower house seat following recount

children.content: The seat of Narungga was so close it required a recount, and has now been won by One Nation candidate Chantelle Thomas by a margin of 58 votes over the Liberals.

image.altText: Chantelle Thomas in a dark jumper looks at camera with a smile in a park

items.title: One Nation candidate published antisemitic, racist and homophobic posts

children.content: A One Nation candidate in the South Australian election was accused of domestic abuse, bullying and harassment, while another made racist, antisemitic and homophobic social media posts, raising fresh questions about the party's vetting processes as it surges in popularity.

image.altText: A collage of screenshots of tweets on top of an image of Tyler Green smiling while posing for a photo.

items.title: Trial pours cold water on current EV truck viability despite diesel price hike

children.content: A 2.5-year trial of an electric truck in SA's forestry industry has concluded with the truck now parked, finding it was able to do the job but wasn't cost effective, even with high fuel prices.

image.altText: A woman wearing a white shirt and glasses looks as the camera, with a lage blue truck in the background.

items.title: Outback SA operator says Easter tourism 'worse than COVID', while others see spike

children.content: An Ikara-Flinders Ranges tourism operator says he is experiencing more cancellations than bookings this Easter as fuel shortage anxiety keep travellers away, while other regional towns expect to be booked out.

gallery.altText: A car drives into the sunset near the Flinders Ranges.

thumbnail.altText: A car drives into the sunset near the Flinders Ranges.

items.title: More GPs charging administration fees to cover payroll tax changes in SA

children.content: Patients are being asked to make an additional, separate transaction at general practices across Adelaide of between $5 and $10 to cover increased operating costs following the implementation of payroll tax for contracted doctors in 2024.

image.altText: A doctor wearing a white coat and a stethoscope writes on a clipboard.

items.title: Fears for stingray populations as algal bloom moves north

children.content: Evidence of South Australia's harmful algal bloom has been detected in previously untouched parts of the Spencer Gulf days after rays were seen gathering in shallow waters.

image.altText: A dead stingray lying in a small puddle among rocks, with its white belly facing up

items.title: 'Everything's dead': How the fuel crisis is hitting small-town Australia

children.content: In one community, the effects of the fuel crisis are not always where you would think.

gallery.altText: Woman with grey hair and black top stands behind cafe counter with hand over register looking out towards window
An illuminated neon sign reflecting red light on a motel that reads Wilson's Prom Motel
A man in a sleeveless blue and white checked shirt sits at a bar holding a can of drink, He has a long white beard and hair.

items.title: Artemis II capsule boost puts astronauts on course to moon

children.content: The Orion capsule carrying four astronauts in NASA's Artemis II mission successfully fired its engines this morning, kicking the crew out of Earth's orbit and bound for the moon.

image.altText: The edge of a white spacecraft is seen on the left. A sliver of the Earth is seen partially covered in darkness on the right.

items.title: Myanmar junta chief Min Aung Hlaing elected president

children.content: In a live broadcast of the vote count the former commander-in-chief comfortably passed the threshold required to win the presidency.

image.altText: Min Aung Hlaing

items.title: Video shows moment Iranian missile strikes Israel as Trump issues further threats

children.content: The ABC verifies video showing the exact moment an Iranian ballistic missile strikes Petah Tikva in central Israel. Follow live.

image.altText: Bright flash amoid skyscrapers in central Israeli city skyline.

gallery.altText: Bright flash amoid skyscrapers in central Israeli city skyline.

items.title: Trump's fiercest female defenders are now on the outer

children.content: Pam Bondi and Kristi Noem were both fired after controversial tenures carrying out Donald Trump's instructions in two of the trickiest roles in his administration.

image.altText: A woman with warm blonde hair looks toward Donald Trump with a stony expression

avatar.alt: Brad Ryan is pictured in front of a news studio background, which shows a bridge in a city.

items.title: Judge dismisses Lively's sexual harassment claims against Baldoni

children.content: A US federal court judge tosses out Blake Lively's sexual harassment claims against It Ends With Us co-star Justin Baldoni, but leaves three more claims intact.

image.altText: A composite image of Baldoni and Lively smiling at the It Ends With Us premiere

items.title: Dezi Freeman's sister details a timeline of horror

children.content: Dezi Freeman's sister says her brother "wreaked havoc and evil" as she opens up on a man who had become lost.

image.altText: Dezi Freeman pictured outside a court in Wangaratta.

items.title: Fresh autumn produce to make the most of in April

children.content: From brussels sprouts to mandarins, these are the autumn fruit and veggies that have sellers excited.

image.altText: A colourful fruit and veggie shop inside Preston Market.

items.title: Cate Blanchett on why her life is 'barely controlled chaos'

children.content: The complexities of family light up Jim Jarmusch's Golden Lion-winner, Father Mother Sister Brother, which features Blanchett alongside Adam Driver and Tom Waits.

gallery.altText: Cate Blanchett wearing a black suit with boxy shoulders, cutouts at the hip and shimmery lapels

thumbnail.altText: Cate Blanchett wearing a black suit with boxy shoulders, cutouts at the hip and shimmery lapels

items.title: Modern life the 'perfect tsunami' for poor liver health

children.content: Heavy drinking on the occasional Saturday night — even if you don't drink at all for the rest of the month — may damage your liver more than previously understood.

image.altText: Happy friends dancing together in a night club

items.title: What to know about cafes using surcharges to offset fuel crisis impact

children.content: Shoppers may have noticed an extra charge at the checkout as businesses seek to offset rising operational costs associated with the fuel price hike. Here is what you need to know.

image.altText: A hand waves a phone showing a credit card app over an eftpos machine.

items.title: How Australia's leaders have changed their language on the Iran war

children.content: With the Strait of Hormuz effectively closed, world oil markets rattled and no obvious off-ramp to the hostilities in sight, the major parties have dialled back their initial optimism.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese stands before a flag.

items.title: Iranian Nobel laureate suffers suspected heart attack in prison, family says

children.content: Iranian activist and Nobel Peace Prize winner Narges Mohammadi has been repeatedly charged and imprisoned by the regime over the past 25 years.

image.altText: A woman with curly hair looks intot he camera

items.title: US military vs Iran's 'arch defence': Dangerous new phase of war looms

children.content: While Donald Trump talks about ending and winning the war in Iran, major US military assets are still on their way to the Middle East. These are the options they give him.

articles.title: Bank of America's $105m settlement with Epstein victims wins preliminary approval

children.content: A US judge has granted preliminary approval to Bank of America's $105.3 million settlement with women who have accused the bank of facilitating their sexual abuse by Jeffrey Epstein.

image.altText: A middle-aged man with grey-white hair wearing a grey T-shirt looks serious in front of a plain background.

articles.title: Pete Hegseth asks the US Army's top uniformed officer to step down

children.content: The ousting comes nearly five weeks into US-Israeli attacks on Iran and with no clear timeline from President Donald Trump on when the war may end.

image.altText: pete hegseth stands next to a us army general at a ceremony

articles.title: Trump puts 100pc tariff on some pharmaceuticals

children.content: The US president's latest order shows he remains committed to his aggressive tariff regime despite February's Supreme Court ruling that invalidated his centrepiece "Liberation Day" tariffs.

image.altText: Donald Trump wearing a suit while speaking into a microphone.

items.title: FIFA raises top ticket price for World Cup final to nearly $16,000

children.content: The top ticket price was $15,900, a sharp increase from the original $12,560 when FIFA sold tickets after the tournament draw in December.

image.altText: Soccer fans gathered around a statue of a FIFA World Cup 2026 ball

items.title: Kerr shoots down Chelsea exit rumour amid US move speculation

children.content: Matildas captain Sam Kerr is quick to cast doubt on a report she will leave Chelsea at the end of the season to join US club Denver Summit.

image.altText: Sam Kerr reacts during Chelsea's Women's Champions League quarterfinal against Arsenal.

items.title: Woods heard telling police he was 'just talking to the president' after crash

children.content: Police have released body camera footage showing Tiger Woods's arrest after he crashed his SUV last week in Florida.

image.altText: A still image from the bodycam footage of Tiger Woods speaking to police after his car crash in Florida.

items.title: A 'rare parasitic fungus' is infecting Top End footballers. Here's what we know

children.content: Health authorities say a rare water-borne pathogen is behind the recent outbreak of severe conjunctivitis in Top End football clubs, with a number of Darwin playing fields deemed high-risk infection sites.

image.altText: a man taking a mark in a football game, splashing in mud.

items.title: A New York Times critic used AI to write a review. This is why it matters

children.content: An author and freelance journalist has admitted to using AI to help him write a book review for the New York Times.

image.altText: Indigenous Tribe NYT Lawsuit

items.title: As more Australian businesses expand into Asia, here's some cultural differences to consider

children.content: In Australia, a good deal builds relationships. In Asia, the opposite is often true. Here are some other cultural differences to understand.

image.altText: group of three men and two women stand around a circular table toasting their glasses.

items.title: Chance of an 'extremely rare' double comet event this month

children.content: Two comets are currently hurtling towards the Sun. If they survive the trip, they may put on a dazzling show for Australian stargazers this month.

gallery.altText: Bright comet with long white tail against a glowing backdrop of the Milky Way with trees at the base of the image.
Starfield with bright comet in the centre, with a bright green head and a more faded tail.
